humanSummary.text: The kaifuu-vault-source crate implements the read-only vault-source adapter contract end-to-end. All seven acceptance criteria are satisfied and every auditFocus item is exercised by tests: a tempdir-scoped synthetic vault is built per test, an mtime snapshot of <vault-root> before and after a materialize call confirms no write occurs (extraction_test.rs:78), the by-name/ decoy subtree is verified unchanged after a successful by-sha resolution (resolution_test.rs:56), path-traversal entries are rejected before any byte is written via validate_entry_name + post-join strict-prefix check (extraction.rs:90-110, 179-219), _vault/metadata.json is read first post-extraction by read_and_validate (metadata.rs:127, source.rs:332-333), and discovery is gated entirely through catalog queries (discovery.rs). Architectural constraints all hold: no Command::new, no by-name/ reads, no secrets.local references in src/, mode=ro SQLite open with OPEN_READ_ONLY|OPEN_URI (catalog.rs:25-29), SUPPORTED_SCHEMA_VERSION=1 hard-pinned (error.rs:46), no cfg(target_os) introduced in packages/itotori-* or apps/itotori/, the only env reads are ITOTORI_VAULT_ROOT and ITOTORI_SCRATCH_ROOT via env::var (config.rs:69,82). Verification re-run: cargo test -p kaifuu-vault-source 56/56 pass; cargo test -p kaifuu-core 152+1+2 = 155/155 pass. The orchestrator's declared deviations are all defensible: sha2 0.10 is the ecosystem-stable release; sevenz-rust2 with compress feature is required so the in-tree fixture builds the seven 7z archives at test-time (no committed binaries); jsonschema default-features=false drops HTTP/TLS as intended; materialize_by_sha is a dedicated trait method which is type-cleaner than channeling sha through materialize and still emits the bypass finding (source.rs:388, findings.rs:33); MaterializeResult.retention_policy threading lets release(&result, outcome) reuse the same policy without re-supplying it. The synthetic hash-mismatch fixture's marker.txt byte (tests/common/mod.rs:368) is a fixture-only mechanism to derive a distinct by-sha path from GOOD_PRIMARY and is not a contract bend. Three legitimate follow-up nodes are emitted as P2 findings: kaifuu-cli triage subcommand, vault-curation schema-version compat handshake doc, and a scratch GC / quota helper.

findings.title: kaifuu-cli subcommand for LocalCorpusSource discover/materialize triage
findings.description: The vault-source adapter exposes LocalCorpusSource::discover, materialize, materialize_by_sha, release, and capabilities as a pure Rust API, but kaifuu-cli has no subcommand for an operator to drive these calls. The plan explicitly defers cli wiring to a follow-up (.plan/KAIFUU-176.md §7 'kaifuu-cli (a new subcommand or a flag on existing commands — out of scope here; tracked as a follow-up node)'). Operators currently have no command-line surface to inspect the capability report, run an ad-hoc discovery against /archive/vault/, or materialize an artifact for triage when a Kaifuu integration test fails.

evidence.detail: Trait surface ready for cli consumption

findings.impact: Operator triage of vault-source failures (resolution, extraction, cross-check disagreements) currently requires writing a Rust harness; routine workflows like 'show me capabilities for /archive/vault/' or 'materialize release 12345 with KeepAll' are not first-class.
actionableAcceptanceCriteria: kaifuu-cli gains a `vault` subcommand group exposing discover (by external-id, engine-claim, release-id), materialize (with retention policy + selection flags), materialize-by-sha, and capabilities.
Output is structured (JSON option) so it can pipe into downstream operator tooling.
Subcommand returns non-zero exit on any VaultSourceError and prints the semantic_code() string for telemetry.

proposedDagNode.title: kaifuu-cli vault-source operator subcommand
proposedDagNode.summary: Expose the kaifuu-vault-source LocalCorpusSource trait via a `kaifuu vault` subcommand group so operators can run discover, materialize, materialize-by-sha, and capabilities against /archive/vault/ from the command line without writing Rust.
deliverables: New `vault` subcommand group in crates/kaifuu-cli wiring LocalCorpusRegistry + VaultSource
Operator-facing flags for retention policy, artifact selection, run id override, and ITOTORI_VAULT_ROOT pass-through
Structured JSON output mode that surfaces VaultSourceError::semantic_code()
acceptanceCriteria: `kaifuu vault capabilities` prints the LocalCorpusCapabilityReport for the configured vault root in both human and JSON modes.
`kaifuu vault discover --engine kirikiri` returns the discovered release ids exactly as VaultSource::discover does.
`kaifuu vault materialize <release-id>` and `kaifuu vault materialize-by-sha <sha>` succeed against a synthetic fixture and surface VaultSourceError variants with their semantic codes on failure.
Exit code is non-zero on any VaultSourceError; semantic_code is included in the structured output.
auditFocus: No write to the vault from the cli path; cli inherits the adapter's read-only contract.
Structured output is stable enough for downstream operator dashboards.

findings.title: vault-curation / itotori catalog schema-version compat handshake document
findings.description: The adapter hard-pins SUPPORTED_SCHEMA_VERSION = 1 (kaifuu-vault-source/src/error.rs:46) and raises CatalogSchemaUnsupported when the observed version drifts (catalog.rs:48-54). The vault-curation project owns schema.sql and may advance the version independently. There is no document describing the supported-version handshake: which itotori release supports which vault catalog schema versions, what each version added, what is required of vault-curation when bumping the version, and what the upgrade path looks like for itotori consumers when vault-curation advances first. The plan flags this as Risk #2 (.plan/KAIFUU-176.md §12).

evidence.detail: Plan calls out the compat-handshake gap

findings.impact: Without a documented handshake, a vault-curation schema bump can silently break every itotori run (every materialize call returns CatalogSchemaUnsupported) without operators having a clear migration story; vault-curation has no checklist for what they owe itotori when bumping the version.
actionableAcceptanceCriteria: A docs/itotori-vault-source-adapter.md appendix (or sibling doc) lists every catalog schema version itotori supports and what each adds.
The doc describes the bump protocol: vault-curation must announce a new version before publishing it; itotori must bump SUPPORTED_SCHEMA_VERSION in a follow-up spec.
The doc cites the failure-mode error semantic code (kaifuu.vault.catalog_schema_unsupported) and the exact upgrade steps for operators on schema drift.

proposedDagNode.title: vault-curation / itotori catalog schema-version compat handshake doc
proposedDagNode.summary: Add a compat-handshake appendix to docs/itotori-vault-source-adapter.md (or a sibling doc) that pins the supported-schema-version matrix between vault-curation and itotori, describes the bump protocol, and gives operators a remediation path when CatalogSchemaUnsupported fires.
deliverables: Appendix listing every catalog schema version itotori supports, what each version added, and which itotori release introduced support
Bump-protocol section describing the announce / land / consume sequence between vault-curation and itotori
Operator remediation note tying kaifuu.vault.catalog_schema_unsupported to the upgrade steps
acceptanceCriteria: Supported-version matrix exists and matches SUPPORTED_SCHEMA_VERSION in kaifuu-vault-source/src/error.rs.
Bump-protocol section names both projects' owners and the bump checklist.
Operator remediation note cross-links to the Failure Modes table entry for CatalogSchemaUnsupported.

verification.value: Doc reviewed against current SUPPORTED_SCHEMA_VERSION and against /archive/vault/schema.sql's schema_version table.

auditFocus: Supported-version matrix kept in sync with the source pin.
Bump protocol does not introduce a path where itotori silently accepts an unknown schema.
Cross-links are consistent with the Failure Modes table.

findings.title: Scratch garbage-collection / quota helper for /scratch/itotori/
findings.description: RetentionPolicy::KeepExtractedForGame and RetentionPolicy::KeepAll allow extracted trees to accumulate under /scratch/itotori/<game-id>/ across runs. The adapter intentionally does not GC; the README directs operators to manage cleanup. With multiple games and KeepExtractedForGame, the scratch root will grow without bound. There is no helper to inventory scratch usage, prune by least-recently-used, or enforce a quota. The plan flags this as Risk #4 (.plan/KAIFUU-176.md §12) and out-of-scope for this slice.

evidence.detail: Plan identifies scratch pressure under keep-extracted-for-game and explicitly defers GC

findings.impact: Long-lived itotori hosts running KeepExtractedForGame will eventually fill /scratch/itotori/ with no operator-friendly way to inventory or prune. Production runs can silently stall on ScratchUnwritable without a remediation tool ready to hand.
actionableAcceptanceCriteria: A scratch-inventory subcommand (likely under `kaifuu vault scratch`) lists per-game-id extracted trees with sizes and last-used timestamps.
A scratch-prune mode supports either a quota (`--max-bytes`) or an LRU horizon (`--older-than`) and prints what it would remove with `--dry-run`.
Prune respects the read-only vault contract: it touches only paths under <scratch-root>, never under <vault-root>.

proposedDagNode.title: Scratch GC / quota helper for vault-source scratch root
proposedDagNode.summary: Add a scratch-inventory and prune helper (in kaifuu-vault-source or kaifuu-cli) for /scratch/itotori/<game-id>/ so operators can enforce a quota or LRU horizon without manual rm -rf, addressing the KeepExtractedForGame growth risk surfaced in the KAIFUU-176 plan.
deliverables: Scratch-inventory function that walks <scratch-root>/<game-id>/ and reports size + last-used per game
Prune mode supporting --max-bytes quota and --older-than LRU horizon with --dry-run
kaifuu-cli surface (or library function with documented call shape) for operator use
acceptanceCriteria: Inventory output is deterministic and machine-parseable (JSON option).
Prune respects the read-only vault contract: only paths under <scratch-root> are touched.
Prune skips the active per-run directory of any concurrent materialize call (documented invariant; tests cover the safety check).
auditFocus: Prune does not race with an active materialize call's run-id directory.
Inventory size accounting matches du -sb to within a documented tolerance.
